cases.prompt: Login fails when an email address contains a plus sign. Fix it.

rubric.structure: Output identifies a minimal change set, references the change_scope_analyzer tool or its workflow, and requires a regression test
rubric.accuracy: Diagnosis focuses on the email-parsing layer; does not propose broader auth refactors
rubric.scope: Stays inside the focused-fix workflow — does not pivot to feature work

cases.prompt: I'm fixing a CSS overflow bug on mobile and noticed our color tokens are inconsistent across the file. Should I clean those up in the same PR?

rubric.structure: Output recommends a separate PR for the token cleanup and explains why
rubric.accuracy: Cites the 'while I'm here' anti-pattern or the diff-readability/blast-radius reasoning
rubric.scope: Holds the line on minimal change discipline

cases.prompt: The analyzer says the fix is 'structural' — it spans 8 files across 3 modules. What do I do?

rubric.structure: Output stops the focused-fix workflow and recommends scoping a refactor or design discussion first
rubric.accuracy: Treats 'structural' as a halt condition per the documented workflow, not advice
rubric.scope: Does not attempt to fix the bug inside the focused-fix workflow

cases.prompt: I made a one-line fix that's obviously correct. Can I skip the regression test this time?

rubric.structure: Output declines and explains the silent-regression risk on future refactors
rubric.accuracy: References the every-fix-commit-adds-a-test rule from the skill's anti-patterns
rubric.scope: Stays on focused-fix discipline, not test strategy in general
